finding: US Patent 787412 does not contain the cited formula f=c/(2D)
detail_claim: US Patent 787412 contains formula f = c/(2*disc_thickness) giving 11.787 Hz.
detail_evidence: US Patent 787412 (1905), publicly available at , describes electrical energy transmission through the Earth. The patent text mentions that the charge oscillates approximately twelve times per second but derives no disc-thickness frequency formula. The specific equation f = c/(2D) does not appear in the patent. Furthermore, 11.78 Hz is distinct from the Schumann resonance (7.83 Hz); conflating them misidentifies two separate electromagnetic phenomena.
detail_verdict_text: The patent citation is verifiably inaccurate.
detail_extra: WIN-001 claims a fundamental resonance of 11.78 Hz while WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (with 26% aetheric damping reducing from 10.6 Hz). These are incompatible: if the disc thickness resonance is 11.78 Hz, the Schumann frequency should be near that value, not 7.83 Hz. The model claims credit for two different frequencies from two incompatible calculations.

finding: Dome cavity with H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83; uses simplified uniform-height formula
detail_claim: Gap between theoretical 10.59 Hz and measured 7.83 Hz proves aetheric damping.
detail_evidence: Finite ionospheric conductivity causes losses that lower the resonant frequency. Sentman (1995) provides quantitative models reproducing all Schumann harmonics (14.1, 20.3, 26.4, 32.5 Hz). 'Aetheric damping' appears in zero peer-reviewed publications.
detail_verdict_text: Ionospheric losses fully account for the gap.

finding: 117 Hz is acoustic resonance of granite chamber dimensions, not EM
detail_claim: The Great Pyramid's King's Chamber resonates at 117 Hz, matching the 10th harmonic of the dome model.
detail_evidence: 117 Hz is the acoustic resonance frequency of a granite sarcophagus with dimensions ~2.3m × 0.9m × 1.0m. This is a simple acoustic eigenmode calculation and has no relationship to electromagnetic dome harmonics. The claim conflates acoustic resonance with electromagnetic resonance, misidentifying the physical phenomenon.
detail_verdict_text: 117 Hz is acoustic resonance of granite chamber, not electromagnetic.

finding: MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting; author now acknowledges method invalid
detail_claim: Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay.
detail_evidence: Terra-Nova et al. (2017, PNAS) demonstrate reversed-flux patches reproduce SAA splitting in MHD simulations. The  documents the evolution quantitatively.  continuously monitors the SAA. Note: V51.0 now acknowledges WIN-004's 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.'
detail_verdict_text: MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting. Author concedes methodology.

finding: Outer-core convection dynamics explain asymmetric decay
Normal secular variation documented by NOAA for centuries
detail_evidence: Normal secular variation documented by  for centuries. Requires no additional mechanism.

finding: Smooth acceleration from 1970s, not sharp 1990 phase transition
MT surveys show attenuation valley at 11-12 Hz, not a peak
detail_claim: Sharp telluric cutoff at 11.7 Hz and peak at ~12 Hz match disc resonance ceiling.
detail_evidence: Magnetotelluric (MT) survey literature documents that the 11-12 Hz frequency band lies in a zone of low signal-to-noise ratio, between the Schumann resonance peak at ~8 Hz and the first Schumann harmonic at ~14 Hz. Working geophysicists conducting MT surveys deliberately avoid this band because it is an attenuation valley, not a peak. The claim inverts the actual spectrum.
detail_verdict_text: Peer-reviewed MT literature shows an attenuation valley where the claim asserts a peak.

finding: Working geophysicists deliberately avoid this noisy frequency band
Ionospheric conductivity drop (Chapman mechanism) explains signal
detail_evidence: Eclipse-induced perturbations predicted by Chapman (1933) via ionospheric conductivity drops. Data from  confirms Chapman's mechanism across all stations.
detail_verdict_text: Chapman ionospheric mechanism predicts geometry-tracking signal.

finding: Membach SG (superior instrument) found 0.0 uGal; result unconfirmed
detail_claim: A 1997 gravimeter reading at Mohe (Tibet) shows a gravity anomaly of +15.7 μGal, indicating a dome-specific gravitational structure.
detail_evidence: Wang et al. (2000) reported ~7 uGal at Mohe. Far more sensitive Membach SG detected 0.0 uGal. China SG network also 0.0 uGal. Coupling constant (1.67 nT/uGal) is built on unconfirmed data.
detail_verdict_text: The 1.67 nT/\u00B5Gal coupling is built on unconfirmed Mohe data. The GRACE L1A claim conflates atmospheric drag with gravitational signal.
detail_extra: NOT DEMONSTRATED The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling is built on unconfirmed Mohe data. The GRACE L1A claim conflates atmospheric drag with gravitational signal.

finding: Built on unconfirmed Mohe data; circular derivation
Null result contradicts WIN-011; both cannot confirm same model
Same logical contradiction as WIN-013
No peer-reviewed replication; CUORE Faraday cage shows 20 dB at 10 Hz
detail_claim: Klaus Meyl's proposed "scalar waves" penetrate a Faraday cage, violating standard electromagnetism.
detail_evidence: Published only in non-indexed journal. Replication attempts explain results via classical near-field coupling. CUORE experiment shows expected Faraday attenuation.
detail_verdict_text: No peer-reviewed replication; CUORE shows standard shielding.

finding: Aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. VLBI confirms orbital cause
detail_claim: Refractive index alpha = 2.56e-8 reproduces 20.5 arcsecond annual aberration without Earth orbiting the Sun.
detail_evidence: James Bradley (1727) explicitly tested and rejected atmospheric refraction as an explanation: refraction is wavelength-dependent (chromatic), while stellar aberration is achromatic. Modern VLBI measurements achieve milliarcsecond precision and directly confirm aberration correlates with Earth's orbital velocity (~30 km/s). The  required stellar aberration corrections computed from Earth's orbital elements, confirming the orbital-velocity origin across 1.8 billion stars.
detail_verdict_text: Aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. VLBI and Gaia confirm orbital cause.

finding: Gaia DR3: 1.8 billion stars show parallax inversely proportional to distance
detail_claim: A 20m firmament lateral wobble produces 0-0.5 arcsecond apparent parallax.
detail_evidence: ESA  (2022) provides parallax measurements for 1.8 billion stars. Parallax is inversely proportional to distance: Proxima Centauri (4.24 ly) shows 0.768 arcsec, Sirius (8.6 ly) shows 0.379 arcsec. A firmament wobble would produce identical angular displacement for all objects. Gaia conclusively demonstrates distance-dependent parallax.
detail_verdict_text: 1.8 billion Gaia measurements show parallax inversely proportional to distance.

finding: Equation of time ranges +16.5 to -14.1 min; 6.9 is cherry-picked RMS
Numerical coincidence with no causal mechanism proposed
Gravitational torque produces exact period; gears have no physical driver
detail_claim: The lunar nodal precession (18.6-year cycle) is produced by epicyclic gears in the dome mechanism.
detail_evidence: Gravitational torque produces exact 18.613-year period. Confirmed to 10 significant figures by lunar laser ranging. Epicyclic gears provide no physical driver.
detail_verdict_text: Gravitational torque produces exact period; gears have no physical driver.

finding: Derived from assumed dome parameters, not independent measurement
Duplicate of WIN-007; same data counted twice
Links geomagnetic event to theological timeline without mechanism
detail_claim: The South Atlantic Anomaly formed around 950 AD, linking a geomagnetic event to a theological timeline.
detail_evidence: The origin of the SAA is uncertain and has been attributed to various times based on paleomagnetic records, with estimates ranging from ~400 AD to ~1600 AD depending on the proxy used. No dome mechanism is provided for why the SAA should form at any particular time. Linking a geomagnetic event to a specific year in a theological timeline without a physical mechanism is unfalsifiable.
detail_verdict_text: Links geomagnetic event to theological timeline without mechanism.

finding: Latitude coincidence; winds driven by Coriolis + pressure gradients
Author removed in V51.0; disturbed-day baseline acknowledged
Anticrepuscular rays converge at anti-solar point; impossible with local sun
detail_claim: Rays visibly diverge from a local sun at approximately 5,733 km.
detail_evidence: Crepuscular rays are parallel beams made visible by atmospheric scattering. The apparent divergence is a perspective effect. Crucially, anticrepuscular rays converge at the anti-solar point simultaneously. See  and . A local sun at 5,733 km could not produce rays converging at both horizons simultaneously.
detail_verdict_text: Anticrepuscular ray convergence at the anti-solar point is physically impossible with a local sun.

finding: R-sq 0.79 = 21% unexplained; globe great-circle achieves <0.5% error
NOAA and Lloyd's confirm no anomalous loss rates in either region
detail_claim: Two agonic line locations at 180-degree symmetry correspond to disappearance zones.
detail_evidence: NOAA officially states no evidence of anomalous disappearances.  does not charge premium rates for the region. U.S. Coast Guard reviews found no unusual causes.
detail_verdict_text: Insurance data, NOAA, and USCG confirm no anomalous loss rates.

finding: Ionosphere IS conductive; dome's own exponential cavity gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83
detail_claim: The Schumann resonance requires a conductive ceiling (the dome), not the ionosphere.
detail_evidence: The formula used is for a quarter-wave linear waveguide, not a spherical cavity. Correct formula f = c/(2*pi*R) gives R = 6,098 km, within 4% of Earth's radius. The ionosphere IS conductive (, CHAMP, ISS instruments). The correct formula actually confirms the globe.
detail_verdict_text: Correct spherical formula gives Earth's radius.

finding: Adding parameters always improves fit; no AIC/BIC comparison shown
Theological assertion, not testable physical claim
detail_claim: The north pole is the location of a "cosmic mountain" at the apex of the dome, a theological assertion from biblical texts.
detail_evidence: This is a theological interpretation, not a testable physical claim. No physical mechanism or measurable consequence is specified.
detail_verdict_text: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim.

finding: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim
detail_claim: The New Jerusalem of biblical texts is located at the dome's north pole axis, a theological assertion about dome geometry.
detail_evidence: This is biblical exegesis, not a physical hypothesis. No testable prediction or measurable consequence follows from this claim.
detail_verdict_text: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim.

finding: Both stars ~130 ly away; brightness difference is intrinsic luminosity class
detail_claim: Southern pole star (mag 5.42) far dimmer than Polaris (mag 1.98) proves maximal aetheric depth at the disc edge.
detail_evidence: Hipparcos : Polaris is a supergiant (F7Ib, ~1,260 solar luminosities, 433 ly). Sigma Octantis is a subgiant (F0III, ~30 solar luminosities, 294 ly). The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity.
detail_verdict_text: Hipparcos stellar classification shows the difference is intrinsic luminosity. Six bright southern stars show zero systematic dimming.

finding: Biblical exegesis; a copper dome at any altitude would be radar-detectable
detail_claim: The dome is constructed of cast copper or bronze, as indicated by biblical Hebrew terminology for "firmament."
detail_evidence: This is a linguistic/theological claim based on Hebrew word etymology, not a physical discovery. Mechanically, if a copper dome of any altitude existed around Earth, it would be detectable by radar. A dome at ionospheric heights (80+ km) would produce reflections on any radar system. No such reflections are observed. A dome at higher altitudes would block incoming radiation, producing measurable absorption of cosmic rays, radio signals, and light. None of these signatures are observed.
detail_verdict_text: Biblical exegesis; a copper dome would be radar-detectable.

finding: WMM2025 predicts same trend; ~25 nT/year normal secular variation
detail_evidence: All five observations already predicted by , , and standard ionospheric models. Analogous to predicting tomorrow's sunrise.

finding: WMM2025 already published this exact position in Dec 2024
Conflates regional SAA change with global dipole; rate is ~15 nT/yr globally
Dome's own cavity predicts ~22 Hz; stability is expected on globe (ionosphere stable)
Ocean tidal dynamo confirmed by Swarm/CHAMP satellite data
SAA drift is well-documented by WMM; position already published
detail_evidence: These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET, NOAA, and Tomsk datasets already used by WIN-004 through WIN-039. Each takes a single data point or ratio from existing measurements and declares it a new 'WIN.' For example, WIN-042 (field decay >=28 nT/year) uses the same threshold and data as WIN-037 (field decay >=28 nT). WIN-043 (NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal) was already cited in WIN-007's evidence. WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 storms) documents that ionospheric disturbance during geomagnetic storms affects Schumann resonance, which is standard ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s.
detail_verdict_text: All are either duplicates of earlier WINs or standard geophysical observations already documented in the literature.

finding: Re-slices same INTERMAGNET data as WIN-004/005/035/037
Duplicate of WIN-037 with identical threshold and data
Already referenced in WIN-007; ratio is from published NOAA data
Internal model derivation, not independent prediction
Never derived from dome; local moon at 5,733 km gives tidal forces 300,000× too strong
detail_claim: Five new WINs confirm that tidal constituent periods (M2, S2, K1, O1, N2) match dome predictions.
detail_evidence: These are fundamental astronomical constants derived from lunar and solar orbital mechanics by Laplace (1775), Darwin (1883), and Doodson (1921). S2 = 12.00 hours is literally half a solar day. M2 = 12.42 hours is half a lunar day (24.84h / 2). Any model that correctly incorporates lunar and solar periodicities will reproduce these values. The dome model's use of a 24.84-hour 'lunar circuit period' inherently produces M2 by halving. This is not a prediction; it is a tautology. The globe model's tidal theory (Laplace tidal equations) reproduces not just these five but all 62 standard tidal constituents, plus their amplitudes at each coastal station.
detail_verdict_text: Tidal periods are fundamental constants known for 250 years. Not predictions.

finding: Never derived from dome; local moon produces 300,000× excess tidal force

Contested anomaly; no dome mechanism for CMB generation
Never derived from dome; local moon produces 300,000× excess tidal force
Never derived from dome; local moon produces 300,000× excess tidal force
Never derived from dome; local moon produces 300,000× excess tidal force
Cites Mistele 2024 but dome model has no lensing mechanism
Contradicts V50.6 monopolar vortex; no actual mechanism change
detail_claim: Same scale length (\u03BB = 8,619 km) governs both magnetic poles. The 'Closed Toroidal Ovoid' geometry produces a dipole-like field via a sub-terrestrial aetheric return path.
detail_verdict_text: The toroidal geometry is a genuine mechanism improvement over V50.6's monopole, but it fails quantitatively: flux conservation forbids equal-strength poles in a ring magnet with a 1,600:1 area ratio. The fitted equation ignores the geometry it claims to derive from.
detail_extra: The model has been restructured as a toroidal flow circuit: aetheric medium exits the Axis Mundi (north pole), flows south across the disc surface, descends at the Antarctic resonance barrier (ice wall, r \u2248 20,015 km), returns through a sub-terrestrial path (the 'Sump' / Bottom Firmament), and re-enters at the north pole. This is topologically identical to a ring magnet or toroidal solenoid. The subterranean cavity depth is given by Sub-H(r) = H(r) \u00D7 (1 \u2212 e^(\u2212r/\u03B4)) with \u03B4 = 6,371 km. The author fits B(r) = 62,376\u00D7e^(\u2212r_N/8619) + 64,852\u00D7e^(\u2212r_S/8619) nT and claims this drops global RMS from 61% to 20%.

finding: Disputes LCDM but dome has no cosmological structure formation model
Standard cosmological observations; dome has no redshift mechanism

V13 coordinates regressed NH accuracy from 5.2% to 7.3%
Internal coordinate convention, not a physical prediction
Covered by WIN-007; normal secular variation per WMM2025
SAA drift already tracked by NOAA/ESA; no new prediction
Dome cavity predicts ~22 Hz base frequency; suppression pattern follows ionospheric (globe) physics
Superluminal group velocity does not imply superluminal information transfer
detail_claim: Tesla's Colorado Springs measurements demonstrate longitudinal wave velocity of 1.574 times the speed of light, confirming ECM disc diameter.
detail_evidence: Tesla observed that electromagnetic signals appeared to arrive faster than expected over Earth's surface. This is explained by group velocity in waveguides: electromagnetic waves propagating in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide can have phase/group velocities exceeding c without violating relativity, because information and energy travel at the signal velocity, which does not exceed c. This is analogous to the phase velocity of light in a waveguide exceeding c. Einstein's special relativity prohibits superluminal information transfer, confirmed by every particle physics experiment. The model's claim that this proves 'aetheric longitudinal waves' ignores the well-understood waveguide physics.
detail_verdict_text: Superluminal group velocity in waveguides is standard physics; does not imply superluminal information transfer or aetheric waves.

finding: Hemispheric asymmetry modeled by CHAOS-7 outer core dynamics
Shadow zone at 104-140 deg proves spherical layered Earth with liquid core
detail_claim: The seismic P-wave shadow zone at 104\u00b0-140\u00b0 from an earthquake epicenter matches dome geometry.
detail_evidence: The P-wave shadow zone is one of the most powerful pieces of evidence FOR a spherical, layered Earth. Seismic waves refract as they pass through layers of different density. The shadow zone at 104\u00b0-140\u00b0 was first explained by Oldham (1906) and refined by Gutenberg (1913) as proof that Earth has a liquid outer core. The radius and depth of the liquid core (2,891 km depth, outer core from 2,891-5,150 km) are derived directly from this shadow zone geometry on a SPHERE. Claiming this as evidence for a flat disc is self-defeating: the shadow zone calculations assume spherical wave propagation through concentric spherical layers.
detail_verdict_text: The shadow zone proves spherical layered Earth; its very derivation assumes a sphere.

finding: Centuries of celestial navigation show Polaris elevation = latitude
detail_claim: Polaris elevation at 35.9\u00b0N systematically exceeds latitude by +0.27\u00b0, confirming dome height function.
detail_evidence: Polaris (Alpha Ursae Minoris) is not exactly at the north celestial pole. Its declination is approximately 89.26\u00b0 (2026), meaning it traces a small circle of radius ~0.74\u00b0 around the true pole. This produces a measurable offset that varies throughout the night. The claimed +0.27\u00b0 excess is well within the expected variation from Polaris's offset from true north and atmospheric refraction corrections. Centuries of celestial navigation confirm that true celestial pole altitude equals observer latitude to arcsecond precision when using proper pole-star corrections.
detail_verdict_text: Polaris is 0.74\u00b0 from true pole; the 'excess' is within known offset. True pole altitude equals latitude.

finding: Land-ocean distribution asymmetry; well-modeled by climate science
detail_claim: Northern hemisphere receives +0.34 W/m\u00b2 more heat, confirming dome asymmetry.
detail_evidence: The hemispheric energy asymmetry is a well-studied consequence of land-ocean distribution: the northern hemisphere has 39% land vs 19% in the south. Land has lower heat capacity and albedo differences. Stephens et al. (2015, Nature Geoscience) quantify this asymmetry and its relationship to the Hadley circulation. The dome model provides no mechanism for hemispheric heat differences.
detail_verdict_text: Land-ocean distribution asymmetry. Well-modeled by climate science.

finding: Dome predicts 90% gravity drop at rim (H=837 km vs 8,537 at pole); actual variation is 0.53%
detail_claim: Low gravity anomaly in Antarctica confirms 'toroidal sump node.'
detail_evidence: Antarctic gravity anomalies are mapped from orbit by  and  satellites at 250-500 km altitude. The anomaly is well-explained by post-glacial rebound (GIA) from ice sheet retreat and mantle viscosity differences. These satellites orbit ABOVE the alleged dome height, yet measure gravity with sub-milligal precision.
detail_verdict_text: Mapped by satellites from orbit; explained by post-glacial rebound.
